title: Chrysanthemum delavayanum
taxonomy: Chrysanthemum delavayanum is classified as an endangered species. It is native to Sikkim, a state in northeastern India, and is considered a rare species. Chrysanthemum delavayanum belongs to the family Asteraceae. This plant is part of the Chrysanthemum genus, which includes many species of flowering plants. intro: Chrysanthemum delavayanum, also known as Delavay's chrysanthemum, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to lower blood pressure, reduce the risk of heart disease, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. The bioactive constituents of Chrysanthemum delavayanum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Chrysanthemum delavayanum is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into herbal supplements and tinctures.
